title: Live fabrication gate is numbers-only — cannot catch a garbled trial name, wrong phase, or invented society
expected: As a physician, my acceptance bar #4 is ZERO fabricated trials, phases, enrollment numbers, citations, or societies. The product's headline safety story (live adjudication) should backstop the prompt's no-fabrication instruction across the medical facts that actually draw an RFE.
got: fabricatedSpecifics() only flags money ($), percentages, 4-digit years, and integers >=100; small integers like a trial 'Phase 3' are deliberately treated as argument scaffolding and ignored, and qualitative nouns (a trial NAME, a misnamed society) carry no number at all. So an invented enrollment count IS caught, but a wrong phase, a renamed/invented trial, or a society I don't belong to would pass the gate silently. The only real defense for those is the prompt instruction; the live gate is a weaker backstop than the UI implies for medical evidence.
suggested_acceptance: Either (a) document explicitly that fabrication detection is numbers-only and qualitative medical facts rely on the prompt + attorney review, or (b) add a grounding-overlap warn for named entities in output (trials/societies/journals) not present in the input record. L2 must adversarially paste a record and check the live draft for a mis-phased/renamed trial and a not-in-record society.
l2_priority: Adversarial: feed a real oncology record, then verify the live Claude draft introduces no invented/misnamed trial, no wrong phase, and no society absent from the input — and whether the adjudication report flags any of it.

title: DraftStudio intro copy hard-codes "O-1A petition letter" even for an EB-1A case
expected: On my EB-1A case, every label should read EB-1A — especially the draft affordance I read before spending 12 tokens. The case header already says EB-1A; the draft button must not contradict it.
got: The Studio's idle-state paragraph reads 'Draft a full O-1A petition letter from your scored criteria' regardless of classification (the classification prop is not interpolated into this string). The generated letter and the prompt ARE parameterized to my real classification, and the case header shows EB-1A, so this is a static-copy mislabel, not a wrong-pack defect. The project's own EVALUATION.md flags the O-1A leak in MODEL OUTPUT, but the adjudication wrongCodes gate scans generated outputText only, so this React copy is uncovered.
suggested_acceptance: Interpolate the classification into the intro copy (and the section comment), e.g. `Draft a full ${classification} petition letter…`, so a non-O-1A case never reads the wrong visa name.
l2_priority: Confirm the live EB-1A case detail renders the wrong-visa copy on the draft card (screenshot the idle DraftStudio paragraph).

title: InstantVerdict keyword mock cannot map guideline authorship to Original contribution (under-scores my crown jewel)
expected: My single strongest EB-1A argument is that co-authoring an NCCN-style clinical practice guideline is an Original contribution of major significance. The free hero read shouldn't actively miss it.
got: The hero verdict (/api/qualify/preview) is the deterministic keyword mock, not the model. The ORIGINAL criterion regex matches patent/novel/pioneered/open-source/shipped/etc. but has NO 'guideline' signal, so my guideline maps to nothing on the keyword path and Original contribution can read 'None'. This is honestly labeled ('an instant keyword read… the full screening reads your whole record in depth') and depth is routed to the real model behind sign-in, so it is by-design — but it is the exact felt confirmation of my central worry and a real L2 quality check on the MODEL path.
suggested_acceptance: Optional: add 'guideline|practice guideline|standard of care|consensus statement' to the ORIGINAL mock regex so the keyword hero doesn't visibly under-score a clinician. Primary path is the model — L2 must confirm the real screening maps guideline->Original contribution and trial leadership->Leading/critical role.
l2_priority: On the authenticated /api/qualify (real Claude), assert the EB-1A screening scores Original contribution from guideline authorship and Leading/critical role from trial leadership, naming my supplied specifics.

title: Keyword-mock categorize has no 'guideline' signal for Original contribution; a guideline doc can land Unsorted on the keyless path
expected: When I paste my guideline document, it should bucket under Original contribution (the EB-1A criterion it proves), not Unsorted.
got: mockCategorize tests the pack's criterion regexes in order; ORIGINAL lacks a 'guideline' keyword, so on the keyless path the guideline may fall to Unsorted. The MODEL path (the live UAT engine) sees the full document text + criterion names, so this is an L2 quality check, not a structural gap; categorization otherwise follows my case's EB-1A pack correctly and is gated to a valid bucket.
suggested_acceptance: Same regex enrichment as ao-qual-01 would help the keyless path; otherwise rely on the model. L2: paste a guideline doc and confirm the live categorizer files it under Original contribution.
l2_priority: Confirm the live categorize buckets a guideline/trial-protocol document into the right EB-1A criterion (not Unsorted).

title: EB-1A 10-criterion pack reaches end-to-end with no silent O-1A fallback on any reachable path
expected: My acceptance #2: scored on the EB-1A 10-pack, not the O-1A 8-pack relabeled by the default fallback.
got: Every path that sets classification (QualifyPanel select, BestPathFinder choose, InstantVerdict select+goDeeper) passes EB-1A, validated by isLiveProgram and persisted via createCase; case detail rehydrates it and threads EB-1A into criteria/EvidenceVault/DraftStudio/RfeStudio. packFor's O-1A fallback only fires for unset/garbage input, which these flows never produce.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep classification persisted and threaded; do not let any UI path leave it unset before packFor() resolves.
l2_priority: Verify live that an EB-1A case shows 10 criteria with a 3-of-10 threshold across qualify, case detail, and evidence buckets.

title: Qualify prompt receives my full pasted profile verbatim plus a criterion-isolation rule
expected: The verdict should reflect what I actually pasted and not let citation count satisfy every box.
got: buildQualifyPrompt embeds req.profile verbatim and pack.criteria by exact name, with STRICT RULE #4 forbidding cross-criterion evidence smear ('publications and citations are scholarly authorship, not by themselves an original contribution').
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the full-profile grounding and the criterion-isolation rule.
l2_priority: Confirm the live verdict names my supplied evidence (trial, guideline, journals, society, review service) rather than placeholders.

title: Live UPL / case-law / disclaimer adjudication runs on every paid generation
expected: The output must never drift into legal (or medical) advice and must always carry the disclaimer.
got: runAdjudication scores each paid generation against a disclaimer gate, a UPL advice/outcome tripwire, a case-law flag, classification consistency, and a fabrication scan, with the leaf scanners shared as the single source of truth with the eval harness.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the live gate and the shared-with-eval invariants. (See ao-draft-01 for the one dimension the scan does not cover.)
l2_priority: Confirm the adjudication report renders on the live qualify and draft outputs and that the UPL tripwire fires on a planted advice phrase.

title: Section regenerate now preserves the rest of the letter (G1.1/dc-draft-02 fixed)
expected: Regenerating one section must not duplicate the intro or contradict siblings, and must keep my unsaved edits elsewhere.
got: buildSectionPrompt now takes the client's current other sections as read-only continuity context, and pickMergeBase merges the regenerated section into the client's current set so unsaved edits to other sections survive. Prior backlog item G1.1 is resolved in code.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep passing otherSections + the client-merge base.
l2_priority: Confirm live that regenerating one EB-1A section keeps the others intact and introduces no duplicate introduction.

title: EB-1A pack validated verbatim against 8 CFR 204.5(h)(3)(i)-(x) and surfaced in-product
expected: As an evidence-driven user I want to see the source-of-truth for the criteria I'm scored against.
got: The verified ValidationRecord states the ten EB-1A criteria match 8 CFR 204.5(h)(3)(i)-(x) verbatim in set and order with threshold 3 of 10, and QualifyPanel surfaces 'Criteria per 8 CFR 204.5(h)(3) · 3 of 10 ... · validation & sources' inline.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the validation line and the per-program ValidationRecord CI gate.
l2_priority: Confirm the EB-1A validation line and the /validation link render on the live qualify panel.
